activities_projects.title: Analyse Judicial Independence
activities_projects.objective: Understand why the judiciary must remain independent from other branches of government
steps: Write the meaning of judicial independence
Explain how independence protects citizens’ rights
Give two examples of situations where independent courts are necessary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the importance of judicial independence
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of explanation and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hierarchy of Courts Case Flow
activities_projects.objective: Understand how cases move through the judicial system
steps: Write the three main levels of courts in India
Draw a flow diagram showing how a case can move from a lower court to the Supreme Court
Explain the idea of appeal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the appellate system in the judiciary
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of diagram and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Public Interest Litigation Research
activities_projects.objective: Understand how PIL helps citizens approach the courts
steps: Research one example of Public Interest Litigation in India
Write what issue was raised in the PIL
Explain how the court’s decision helped the public
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects judiciary functions with real-life cases
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mock Court Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand how a court hearing works
steps: Create a simple case scenario in class
Assign roles like judge, lawyer, and witness
Conduct a short mock trial and give a verdict
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of roles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Protection of Rights
activities_projects.objective: Analyse how courts protect Fundamental Rights
steps: Write about a situation where someone’s rights are violated
Explain how the person can approach the court
Explain how the judiciary can restore justice
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies judicial principles to real-life situations

activities_projects.title: Compare Civil and Criminal Justice
activities_projects.objective: Understand different types of legal cases
steps: Write the meaning of civil and criminal cases
List two differences between them
Give one example of each type
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical understanding of legal systems

activities_projects.title: Role of Evidence in Justice
activities_projects.objective: Understand how evidence influences court decisions
steps: Write what evidence means in law
List different types of evidence used in courts
Explain how evidence helps judges make fair decisions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the importance of evidence in the justice system

activities_projects.title: Judiciary and Democracy Essay
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between judiciary and democracy
steps: Write a short essay explaining why courts are important in a democracy
Explain how the judiciary protects equality and justice
Include examples from India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the democratic role of the judiciary
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of essay (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Study a Landmark Judgement
activities_projects.objective: Understand the impact of important court decisions
steps: Find one important Supreme Court judgement
Write what the case was about
Explain how the judgement affected society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises the influence of judicial decisions

activities_projects.title: Critical Thinking: Justice in Society
activities_projects.objective: Encourage analysis of justice in real life
steps: Write three situations where people may need to go to court
Explain how the judiciary can solve these issues
Explain why equal justice is important for society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the concept of justice to everyday situations
